description: Per-POS export column dictionary. This is the single most defensible asset in OpticAI: the encoded knowledge of exactly how Toast, Square, and Clover name and structure their exports, including the gotchas. A weekend clone has none of this and will mis-map columns the moment a real export is uploaded. Expand this with every new export you see in the wild — it compounds.

toast.label: Toast POS

detection_signatures.comment: How to recognize a Toast export from its headers. If 2+ signature columns are present, classify as Toast.

gotchas: Toast 'Net Sales' is already after discounts and comps — do NOT subtract comps again.
Toast labor exports are a SEPARATE report from sales; they will arrive as different CSVs and must be joined on Business Date + Location.
Toast 'Sales Category' is the reporting group, which may differ from the kitchen menu category.
Multi-location Toast accounts sometimes export one file per location with no location column — the location is in the filename.
A column labeled 'COGS' is usually TOTAL COGS (food+beverage+paper), not Food Cost alone — map to total_cogs and confirm before using as food cost.

square.label: Square
gotchas: Square 'Transactions' = checks/tickets, NOT covers. Square does not natively track guest count — per-person metrics must fall back to per-check.
Square 'Net Sales' = Gross Sales - Discounts - Comps - Refunds; tax is separate.
Square labor data comes from Square Shifts/Payroll, a different export with columns like 'Total Paid' and 'Regular Hours'.
Square exports often split into separate 'Items', 'Categories', and 'Sales Summary' files — they must be reconciled.
A column labeled 'COGS' is usually TOTAL COGS (food+beverage+paper), not Food Cost alone — map to total_cogs and confirm before using as food cost.

clover.label: Clover
gotchas: Clover often exports at the ORDER or LINE-ITEM grain, not pre-aggregated by day — expect to roll up to business date yourself.
Clover timestamps are full datetimes; truncate to business date and apply the business_day_close_hour rule for late-night.
Clover 'Revenue' vs 'Net Revenue' distinction is inconsistent across merchant configs — verify which is post-discount.
Clover has weak native labor reporting; labor usually comes from a third-party integration with its own schema.
A column labeled 'COGS' is usually TOTAL COGS (food+beverage+paper), not Food Cost alone — map to total_cogs and confirm before using as food cost.

generic.label: Unknown / Generic CSV
gotchas: No POS signature matched. Fall back to the ontology synonym map and raise confirmation prompts more eagerly.
Treat all ambiguous columns (transactions, count, covers) as requiring user confirmation.
